channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: A number of dangerous potholes that trigger the vehicle anti skid and traction control, 14th May
item.description:
Her GCHQ, Turning right out of Telstar way onto the A40 going west . THESE HAVE BEEN HERE FOR WEEKS AND ARE UNSAFE.

item.title: Light is not working., 14th May
item.description:
The lamp post with the faulty light is on the footpath outside properties 25 [mine], 22, 23 and 19 Buckles Close, all at postcode GL53 8QT. The issue has been going for a couple of days. Kind regards

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25, Buckles Close, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2) large pothole, unsure on depth but getting worse., 14th May
item.description:
Outside Lower Laynes, as plotted using pin.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Aggs Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple small potholes, 14th May
item.description:
Outside number 15 lypiatt street, and running for a span of around 10m. These are routinely fixed, poorly and individually. Opened up again for the umpteenth timw because they are never dealt with as a whole. It needs one single large covering over all of the cracks and fixing them piecemeal leaves many little holes and fissures for the rain and tyre pressire to start the process all over again.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Princes Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 1) large pothole that is substantially worse since inspection - may have damaged vehicle, 14th May
item.description:
Outside Lower Laynes - as plotted
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Aggs Hill, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree over hanging road, 14th May
item.description:
Tree hanging low over road. Looks like it could come down, potentially on someone, especially if a lorry, tall van clips it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A40, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous obstacle, 14th May
item.description:
The council installed new bins and moved their location. Previously hidden, this sharp metal end from a previous road sign is harmful for all pedestrians or cyclists. In front of The London Inn pub where the double yellow lines start.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 201, London Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Long tyre track width pothole, 14th May
item.description:
Just as you come off the roundabout from Shurdington Road there is a long tyre track pothole on the roundabout.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shurdington Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham

Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Tree causing damage/obstruction to pavement or road

item.title: INADEQUATE CLEARANCE & FAILED INSPECTION: Critical Skid Hazard Remains, 13th May
item.description:
I am updating this report following a partial attempt by the council to clear the lane yesterday. While I appreciate that approximately 200kg of grit was removed, this has barely addressed the scale of the problem. The crew on-site explicitly stated that significantly more work is required but couldn’t stipulate when or for sure. **1. Continuing Safety Risk:** Despite the partial sweep, a substantial layer of fine silt and loose grit remains across the steep gradient and blind junction. This continues to act as a "ball-bearing" skid hazard for vehicles and cyclists. A 4-year-old child has already been injured here; leaving the job half-finished is a failure of the Highway Authority’s duty of care. **2. Inadequate Safety Inspection:** A Highways Safety Inspector attended the site today. However, they were observed performing a three-point turn and leaving without a thorough foot inspection. Critically, at the time of their visit, a vehicle was parked over the largest accumulation of debris, meaning the inspector could not have accurately assessed the depth of the silt or the severity of the skid risk. **3. Health and Property Impact:** As the weather remains dry, the remaining silt is being whipped up into clouds of fine dust. We are still unable to open our windows due to the respiratory health risk and the threat of damage to sensitive computing equipment within our home. **Required Action:** This cannot be closed as "resolved." I require a full mechanical sweep to be completed immediately to remove the remaining hundreds of kilograms of debris. I also request a re-inspection on foot when the road surface is clear of parked vehicles to ensure the "intervention criteria" for a hazardous road surface are correctly met. I am keeping a log of this correspondence for escalation to the Local Government Ombudsman should this jurisdictional buck-passing continue to compromise public safety.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Street, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped items, 13th May
item.description:
Black bag, cone, sign dumped in the corner of the car park area for the houses on Stearman Walk, access to the car park is via Roselle Drive then Linden Close Brockworth.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Linden Close, Hucclecote, Tewkesbury
